name: Roland Hanna
abstract: Roland Pembroke Hanna (February 10, 1932 – November 13, 2002) was an American jazz pianist, composer, and teacher.

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Tabackin
about_quotes.evidence: Lew has played with Elvin Jones, Roland Hanna, Donald Byrd and with a number of television studio bands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Tabackin interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Roland Hanna on the piano... myself. And we were the young musicians in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: And then Roland Hanna, he came in after Tommy Flanagan and played with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: He was a member of the New York Jazz Sextet and we would play many duo things in the process of the concert. We would have Ron Carter and Billy Cobham lay out and it would just be me and Roland playing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Harry Edison
about_quotes.evidence: Roland played with you for…it wasn’t just that one period. He stayed with you for a while. ... Yes, he played with me off and on.
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry Edison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: Again, another one of my friends who's gone way too soon... I did some Japanese tours with Roland, with my band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
